sum of the case-mix and wage-adjusted 30-day period payment and the wage-
adjusted fixed dollar loss amount (sum of Steps 1 and 2 - outlier threshold); 
 5. Multiply the difference by the loss sharing ratio; and 
 6. That total amount is the outlier payment for the 30-day period. 

Effective January 1, 2010, an outlier cap precludes any HHA from receiving more than 10 
percent of their total home health payment in outliers.
